questions.stem: Which of the following best describes ethnocentrism?

choices.text: Patterns or traits found in all known human cultures.

choices.label: B
choices.text: A culture's shared standards of what is good, desirable, and worthy.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Widespread and exaggerated public fear that some group or behavior threatens society.

choices.label: D
choices.text: The tendency to evaluate other cultures by the standards of one's own culture, seen as superior.

questions.explanation: Ethnocentrism is best described as the tendency to evaluate other cultures by the standards of one's own culture, seen as superior. The other options describe different concepts.

questions.stem: Which of the following is the best example of a subculture?

choices.label: A
choices.text: A community of skateboarders with their own slang, dress, and unspoken etiquette.

choices.label: B
choices.text: A person turning to theft after finding no lawful path to the wealth society prizes.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Sweeping public alarm that a new video game is corrupting the nation's youth.

choices.label: D
choices.text: A large coordinated campaign pressing lawmakers for new environmental protections.

questions.explanation: An example of a subculture is: a community of skateboarders with their own slang, dress, and unspoken etiquette. In general, a subculture refers to a group within a society that shares distinctive norms and values while sharing the larger culture. The other options illustrate different concepts.
